United Kingdom
 
Full job description
A rare and exceptional role has emerged at one of Ireland's most exciting new luxury historic castle in the Midlands, opening its doors to private clientele for the very first time. This is not a conventional general manager role. It is something altogether different.

As Castle Manager, you will be the beating heart of this property - part host, part leader, part guardian of an extraordinary heritage. Operating within an ultra-luxury, privately hosted model, you will curate deeply personal experiences for guests.

THE OPPORTUNITY

Shape the identity of a brand-new Irish luxury castle from the very beginning
Lead a hand-picked, elite team within an intimate, high-touch environment
Deliver flawlessly personalised guest experiences with no two days the same
Become the custodian of a genuine piece of Irish heritage and history
Position a new property at the very top of the Irish luxury hospitality market
WHAT YOU'LL DO

Oversee all day-to-day castle operations - accommodation, dining, grounds and events
Be personally present at key guest moments: arrival, dinner, departure
Lead pre-arrival guest profiling and bespoke journey planning
Maintain immaculate presentation and operational standards throughout
Recruit, coach and develop a small, cross-trained high-performance team
Build relationships with luxury travel advisors and key local partners
Ensure full regulatory, safety and compliance standards are upheld
WHO YOU ARE

You bring 3-4+ years of senior luxury hospitality leadership, ideally within a castle, country house, or ultra-luxury boutique property. You understand that at this level, emotional intelligence is as important as operational precision. You are calm under pressure, instinctively hospitable, and deeply discreet.

Experience with Forbes 5-Star, LQA, or Michelin 3 Keys standards is highly advantageous, as is a background in pre-opening properties or exclusive-use venues.

PREFERRED BACKGROUND

Full-service luxury castles or private country houses
Ultra-luxury boutique hotels or private residence director roles
Exclusive use / private hire properties for weddings and events
International luxury hospitality - Ireland experience a plus

shortRoleReason: Role title is clearly stated as Castle Manager.
companySummary: The iNUA Collection is hiring for a luxury castle property role within its hospitality portfolio.
roleSummary: Senior hospitality leader for a new luxury historic castle, responsible for operations, guest experience, team leadership, and standards in a privately hosted model.
headlineAttraction: Shape a brand-new luxury castle from the beginning and lead an elite team in a privately hosted, ultra-luxury setting.
rolePurpose: To oversee the full operation of a luxury castle while delivering deeply personalised guest experiences and maintaining exceptional standards.
advertSummary: A full-time Castle Manager role with The iNUA Collection for a new luxury historic castle in the Midlands, focused on ultra-personalised guest experiences, operational leadership, and pre-opening style ownership of a high-end heritage property.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right at the point where operations, guest experience and standards have to work together without friction. My background is in live, high-pressure environments where service quality, control and team discipline all matter at once, and I’ve built a habit of making those moving parts easier to run.
whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I do best: taking ownership of a complex operation, setting clear standards, and making sure the guest experience feels personal rather than process-led. I’m comfortable being close to the detail, close to the team, and close to the moments that matter to guests.

My background is in live, high-pressure environments where service quality, control and team discipline all matter at once, and I’ve built a habit of making those moving parts easier to run.

That combination of newness, exclusivity and responsibility is what makes it stand out to me.

I’m comfortable being close to the detail, close to the team, and close to the moments that matter to guests.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Delivering a consistently strong guest experience
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I turned around a live patient-facing service where perception and follow-through mattered, and I improved the external experience in a measurable way. That is relevant here because guest experience at this level depends on consistency, responsiveness and attention to detail.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest guest-experience proof point near the top of the page.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I improved a live customer experience score from 2.09 to 4.51.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Maintaining standards, safety and operational control
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a practical tracking system in a safety-critical leisure environment, which shows I’m comfortable putting structure around live operations. That matters for a castle operation where standards, compliance and team readiness all need to stay visible.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting proof for standards, safety and day-to-day control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I put a clearer compliance tool in place in a safety-critical operation.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Leading a small team through growth and change
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase, where delivery discipline, resilience and team coordination all had to improve together. That translates well to a new property that needs strong habits built early.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as evidence of operational grip and team leadership under growth pressure.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I helped strengthen margin, orders won and resilience during scale-up.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: This role seems to need someone who can hold the whole operation together while still making the guest experience feel personal and discreet. It also needs someone who can build standards early, lead a small team well, and stay calm when the pace changes or the detail gets heavy.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I improved a live service environment by tightening follow-through and lifting external feedback significantly.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can improve how guests or clients feel about the operation, not just how it runs.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Operational oversight across multiple moving parts
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve run day-to-day operations in a scale-up setting where production, people, systems and customer requirements all needed close control.
experienceMappings.relevance: Useful for a castle operation covering accommodation, dining, grounds and events.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a tracking system to monitor operating hours and support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical environment.
experienceMappings.relevance: Directly relevant to maintaining control in a high-standard hospitality setting.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations through growth where the team needed clearer ways of working and stronger delivery discipline.
experienceMappings.relevance: Matches the need to recruit, coach and develop a small cross-trained team.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve supported business-critical delivery in a high-security, data-sensitive environment while managing workload and service standards.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the level of discretion and steadiness the role asks for.

focusAreasToBring.title: Guest moments
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on the arrival, dinner and departure points that shape how the property is remembered, and make sure those moments feel deliberate rather than improvised.

focusAreasToBring.title: Standards from day one
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would set clear routines around presentation, compliance and service follow-through so the property feels controlled and consistent as it grows.

focusAreasToBring.title: Small-team performance
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would build a team rhythm that keeps expectations clear, coaching practical and accountability simple, because a small team has to work cleanly to deliver this kind of experience.

focusAreasToBring.title: Operational visibility
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on the basics that make the whole operation work: workload, handovers, standards, and anything that could affect guest confidence.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background sits well with a role that needs both operational control and a strong guest lens. I’ve worked in live environments where standards, service and team discipline all had to hold together, and I’m used to bringing order to complexity without losing pace.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months getting close to the property’s rhythm, the guest journey and the team’s working habits. I’d focus on making the operation dependable, helping shape the service style around the private-client model, and putting practical structure around standards, handovers and compliance.
companyHighlights: Heritage property with a strong sense of place
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where the detail matters, the standards are high, and people expect calm judgement rather than noise. The brief suggests exactly that kind of setting, and that suits how I operate.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the guest journey, the team, the operational routines and the standards already in place, so I understand what needs protecting and what needs shaping.
I would work on handovers, service consistency, presentation standards and the practical controls that keep a small luxury operation steady.
I would focus on coaching, accountability and guest-facing consistency, while making sure the operation is ready to deliver reliably at the level the brief describes.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring calm control, strong guest focus and a practical approach to building a new luxury operation well.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live customer experience score from 2.09 to 4.51.
I built a compliance-tracking system in a safety-critical leisure environment.
I’ve led day-to-day operations through growth and change in complex settings.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct castle, country house, Forbes, LQA or Michelin 3 Keys experience because it is not present in the CV or evidence bank.
I have used the strongest available hospitality-adjacent evidence from NHS primary care, leisure operations and manufacturing to support the fit.
The advert references Ireland and Midlands inconsistently; I have stayed with the company name and role context rather than adding location claims beyond the brief.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
